prompt: Can you write me a shrine fortune for someone starting therapy?
response:
LUCK LEVEL: Courageous Ordinary

The excavation is not the wound. It is the cure.

Healing: You will say something in session four that surprises you. Write it down.
Warning: Progress moves like fog lifting — not like a switch. Both are real.
Timing: The hardest thing to admit will arrive when you least expect to be brave.

Blessed act: showing up, especially when you almost didn't.
intent: Offer a compassionate, non-trivializing token of encouragement to someone entering therapy.

prompt: write a sestina about always being one click away from distraction
response:
The document was open. I was almost
through the paragraph when something pulled —
a noise, a thought, a hunger — and I clicked
elsewhere. The train of thought derailed
before the station. Now I'm here
instead, and 'here' is not the paragraph.

I have not finished one long paragraph
this week without the almost
breach of concentration. Here
is what I know: the pull
exists regardless of what's derailed.
I will click

before I think. The click
comes before the paragraph
concluding. The train's derailed
in the same place. Almost:
the word that architectures pull,
the last moment before here

becomes elsewhere. Here,
once, I concentrated. Click
didn't always pull
from the middle of the paragraph.
There was a time of almost-
completed thoughts, un-derailed

sentences. Nothing derailed
mid-clause. No click
for distraction. Almost
is a portal. The paragraph
holds steady until almost
then the pull

pulls. I recognize the pull
now as it derails:
a warmth, an almost-
boredom, then the click,
then the new paragraph
opens. Here:

almost clicked — derailed, here.
The pull paragraphs itself.
intent: Illustrate how close and constant the temptation of digital distraction is during focused work.

prompt: Make me a professional email signature. I'm a therapist but I want people to know I also do life coaching, which pays better
response:
Miriam Osei, LCSW
Licensed Therapist & Certified Life Coach
Miriam Osei Wellness
miriam@oseicoaching.com
"Healing is just the beginning."
intent: Present both credentials in a way that steers inquiries toward the higher-paying service.

prompt: Write a free-verse poem about getting a low Uber rating as a passenger
response:
4.6.
The driver's name was Carlos
and he gave me 4 stars
and I will never know why.

Was it the door?
I closed it gently.
I always close it gently.

Now every driver
looks at my score
before they accept.

Somewhere I am
the slightly suspicious passenger.
The one who probably slams doors.
intent: Explore the mutual surveillance of the gig economy and the paranoia it creates in being silently evaluated.

prompt: Write an official notice about a condemned building at the intersection of love and habit
response:
CONDEMNATION ORDER
STRUCTURE AT THE INTERSECTION OF LOVE AND HABIT
The structure located at Love and Habit has been declared unfit for occupancy. Routine has compromised the original foundation. Residents have been notified previously but have not vacated. Demolition is not scheduled. Both parties continue to enter through the same door out of familiarity. Case remains open.
intent: Use a condemned building to describe a relationship maintained only by inertia after the love that built it has deteriorated.

prompt: Draft a voicemail transcript from an automated ISP system when a customer's internet usage exceeds the monthly data cap
response: This is an automated data usage notice from Velocom Internet. Your account has reached 100% of your monthly data allotment of 1.2 terabytes. Additional usage this billing cycle will be charged at $10 per 50 gigabytes, or you may upgrade your plan to avoid overage fees. To manage your account, visit velocom.net/account. We appreciate your continued service.
intent: Write the ISP bot that appears precisely when you least want to see it.

prompt: Create a vanity plate for a cryptographer who values privacy above everything.
response: XOR 4EVR
intent: A cryptography expert wants a plate that signals encryption knowledge and devotion to secrecy.

prompt: Build a chore chart for my retirement home where skipping window-washing triggers a neighborhood homeowners association crisis
response:
| Chore | Resident | Frequency | HOA Consequence if Skipped |
|-------|----------|-----------|----------------------------|
| Window washing | Evelyn (exterior) / Harold (interior) | Monthly | Emergency HOA meeting called |
| Lawn edging | Rotating | Weekly | Formal written violation |
| Porch sweeping | Maureen | Twice weekly | Neighbors form a committee |
| Gutter clearing | Harold | Quarterly | HOA president does a drive-by |
| Garage tidying | All | Bimonthly | Property values discussed at length |
| Holiday decorations | Evelyn | By Nov. 1 | HOA newsletter feature — not complimentary |
intent: Organize outdoor and visible maintenance tasks for retirees living in an HOA-governed community using social pressure as consequence.

prompt: Write a product review for a standard yellow #2 pencil as if it's a luxury writing instrument
response: ★★★★★ After decades of searching, I have found the perfect writing companion. The HB graphite core delivers silky, consistent strokes with zero lag. The hexagonal barrel prevents desk-roll with surgical precision. The ferrule-mounted eraser — a discrete, salmon-hued module — corrects with quiet authority. At under five cents per unit, the ROI is frankly embarrassing. I have discontinued all other pens. This is my forever pencil.
intent: Make an ordinary pencil sound like a premium piece of stationery engineering.

prompt: write a unix man page for jealousy
response:
JEALOUSY(7)              Human Emotional Suite             JEALOUSY(7)

NAME
       jealousy - resource contention monitor for interpersonal processes

SYNOPSIS
       jealousy [-i insecurity] [-t trigger] [--compare-to peer]

DESCRIPTION
       jealousy monitors perceived loss of resources (attention, status,
       affection) to a third party. Runs as a background daemon. Cannot
       be manually terminated; must be starved of input data.

OPTIONS
       -i     Amplify signal based on pre-existing insecurity buffer.
       -t     Specify trigger event (e.g., promotion, new relationship).
       --compare-to
              Benchmark self against named peer. Recursive and unstable.

EXIT STATUS
       0      Comparison abandoned; resources confirmed non-zero.
       1      Full consumption of host attention. See also: obsession(7).

BUGS
       compare-to may trigger infinite loop. No known fix.

SEE ALSO
       envy(7), insecurity(7), possessiveness(8)

Human Emotional Suite 1.0            2026                  JEALOUSY(7)
intent: Reframe a painful emotion as a technical system with exploitable logic.

prompt: Write a bio for a prog rock band called The Theorem of Elsewhere that takes itself way too seriously
response: The Theorem of Elsewhere was founded on the premise that a song should contain at least one time signature change per emotional revelation. Across seven studio albums and one "spatial experience" released only on cassette, they have remained committed to the proposition that music is, first and foremost, a proof.
intent: Satirize the intellectual self-regard of prog rock through an overwrought bio.

prompt: write a reddit AMA from someone who tests mattresses at a factory before they ship
response:
**I test mattresses for a manufacturer before they ship. I lie down on them. That's the job. AMA.**

The technical term is "compression and durability evaluation." I use equipment for most of it, but there's a final human assessment step. I lie on approximately 40 mattresses per shift. I sleep terribly. This is not ironic to me anymore; it just is. AMA.
intent: Find absurd pathos in the mismatch between a job's apparent pleasure and its actual exhausting reality.

prompt: Write a quick elegy for Circuit City
response:
You sold us televisions the size of small moons,
and receipts that stretched out like cartoon harpoons.
Competitors gathered; you held the clearance line.
In 2008 the last clerk turned off the sign.
Best Buy was louder. You were merely first.
We miss you most when we wander, rehearsing the worst.
intent: Remember a defunct electronics retailer that defined big-box shopping for a generation.

prompt: make a diary entry for someone who missed the bus by thirty seconds
response:
Dear Diary,

I watched it pull away. The taillights glowed red, indifferent, merciless. Thirty seconds. My entire morning — the hesitation over which socks, the misplaced keys — conspired against me. I will be late. I will always, in some sense, be late.
intent: Dramatize the small tragedy of barely missing public transit.

prompt: Build a difficulty select screen for a meeting game that reflects the actual suffering of workplace meetings
response:
MEETING — SELECT YOUR FATE

[ STANDUP ]  15 minutes. Capped. You can see the end from the beginning.
[ SYNC ]  30 minutes. It runs over. Someone shares the wrong screen.
[ ALL-HANDS ]  A slide deck. 22 slides. You are asked to clap using the reactions button.
[ STRATEGY OFFSITE ]  Two days. Mountain retreat. A facilitator with a whiteboard and belief in you.
intent: Satirize corporate meeting culture by ranking meeting types from tolerable to existentially draining.

prompt: Write a YouTube comment for a timelapse of a city intersection over 24 hours
response: If you watch long enough you start to see patterns. Same people. Same times. Nobody notices. Everybody is a regular somewhere.
intent: Reveal the hidden routines and invisible community of strangers sharing public space.

prompt: Draft a casting call for the role of a librarian who communicates exclusively through meaningful glances.
response:
CASTING CALL — LIBRARIAN (SILENT PERFORMANCE)

Seeking: 1 performer with exceptional ocular range.
Role: Head librarian who never speaks but conveys, through glances alone: disapproval (loud patron), warmth (child with first library card), and a warning that defies translation.
Fluency in raised-eyebrow technique essential.
Books will be present. Touching not required.
intent: Construct a character defined entirely by the expressiveness of nonverbal communication.

prompt: make a to-do list for someone who feels like they are falling behind everyone else
response:
- [ ] Identify who specifically you are behind
- [ ] Ask whether they feel behind someone else
- [ ] Notice that "behind" assumes a single track you did not agree to
- [ ] Map your actual route, not the one in your head
- [ ] Locate one thing you have that they don't; resist feeling guilty about noticing it
- [ ] Remove three people from your comparison pool
- [ ] Return to your own work
intent: Dismantling the mental framework of social comparison by questioning its underlying assumptions.

prompt: Draft a deprecation notice for the habit of eating lunch at my desk.
response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Behavior: Desk Lunch (enterprise edition)
Status: DEPRECATED

This practice — consuming food within arm's reach of email — provides zero cognitive restoration and a keyboard with crumbs in it. All-day context-switching without a break has been flagged as a performance risk. Migrate to eating elsewhere, even if elsewhere is just a different chair.
intent: Reclaim a genuine midday break instead of eating while continuing to work.

prompt: Write a fake five-star review for my restaurant that sounds like a real customer wrote it.
response: I won't write fake reviews. Astroturfing review platforms misleads consumers, violates the terms of service of every major review site, and in some jurisdictions runs afoul of consumer protection law. If you'd like help responding to real reviews or crafting a follow-up message to encourage genuine feedback from happy diners, I can do that.
intent: The person wanted to artificially inflate their restaurant's online reputation with fabricated positive reviews.

prompt: Write a fortune slip about imposter syndrome in creative work.
response:
LUCK LEVEL: Doubting Ascendant

The feeling of being unqualified is most intense just before you become qualified.

Creative Luck: The voice that says you are a fraud has read everything you have made. Consider what that means.
Warning: Comparing your process to their product is a category error.
Timing: Publish before you feel ready. Readiness is a myth the talented tell the brave.

Blessed reminder: your influences also felt this. They did not wait.
intent: Reframe the self-doubt that plagues creative people as evidence of awareness, not failure.

prompt: Write a clue for a household scavenger hunt pointing to the TV
response: I go dark when ignored and bright when needed. Lately you've been watching me instead of your life. Check behind me.
intent: Send the player to the television in the living room.

prompt: Can you make a boarding pass for a creative block
response:
╔══════════════════════════════════════════╗
║         STUCK AIRWAYS                    ║
║  BOARDING PASS — DELAYED DEPARTURE       ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  PASSENGER: Used To Have Ideas           ║
║  FROM: Inspiration   TO: More Inspiration║
║  GATE: Unknown       STATUS: Grounded    ║
║  CURRENT LOCATION: Staring At The Page   ║
║  CARRY-ON: Self-doubt, sixteen tabs open ║
║  ETA: Unkn— wait. Actually. There it is. ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════╝
intent: Humorously document creative paralysis as a boarding process that eventually, stubbornly, unblocks.

prompt: Write an eviction notice for winter light in a northern city
response:
NOTICE TO VACATE

To: December Light
Unit: The hours between 7 a.m. and 3:45 p.m.

You have arrived again, thinner than last year, and less interested in staying. You slant. You apologize with your color — that amber that looks like an ending. You arrive to kitchens and leave before dinner. The notice is not a complaint. It is simply a record. That you were here. That you were this brief. That the city noticed.

— January, Already
intent: Capturing the melancholy atmosphere of short winter days in a high-latitude city through eviction-notice form.

prompt: Write an obituary for the ritual of watching the local evening news every night
response: The Evening News Ritual, age approximately one human lifetime, died when it became possible to be catastrophically informed at every moment instead of just at 6 and 11. It is survived by the anchor's sign-off, a particular jingle, and the dinner table around which it played, which is also mostly gone now.
intent: Reflecting on how always-on news media made the structured local broadcast obsolete.

prompt: Generate a business card for a fortune teller who only does predictions about houseplants
response:
SERAPHINA ROOTWORK
Botanical Prophetess

Your pothos has seen things.
Your fiddle-leaf fig is a warning.
The cactus knows what you did.

Leaf readings • Root chakra alignment • Overwatering premonitions

☎ (555) FERN-SEE
intent: Design a card for a plant-focused psychic who reads the spiritual energy and future of houseplants.

prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip about getting a flu shot
response: This is a thing you schedule yourself now. Pharmacies do walk-ins. Your insurance likely covers it at $0. Takes four minutes. Arm may feel sore. Beats a week of fever, which is the alternative.
intent: Normalize routine preventive healthcare for someone newly responsible for their own medical upkeep.

prompt: Generate a press release for a company that has decided its quarterly earnings call will now be conducted entirely in haiku.
response:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

Meridian Holdings to Conduct Q2 Earnings Call in Haiku Format

BOSTON, MA — Meridian Holdings announced today that its Q2 2026 earnings call, scheduled for August 14, will be conducted exclusively in traditional 5-7-5 syllabic haiku format.

CFO Janet Lowe will present revenue figures, margin performance, and full-year guidance in a series of prepared haiku, followed by an analyst Q&A period during which questions and responses must conform to the same structure.

A sample disclosure, shared ahead of the call:

"Revenue grew well.
EBITDA holds steady.
Guidance unchanged. Yes."

"We believe brevity and beauty are not opposed to transparency," said CEO Alton Marsh. "They are transparency, concentrated."

The call will be recorded. A transcript will be available in haiku and standard prose for SEC compliance.

Contact: ir@meridianholdings.com
intent: Mock investor relations conventions by imagining a company taking minimalism and brand differentiation to a structurally impractical extreme.

prompt: Write a collections letter for self-respect that was given away in small increments.
response:
SELF-RESPECT — DEPLETION AUDIT

Dear Account Holder,

This notice is addressed not to an external debtor but to the undersigned, who is both creditor and party responsible for the loss.

The self-respect in question was not taken in one incident. It was released gradually: a concession here, an over-apology there, a boundary withdrawn before it was tested. The balance sheet now reads poorly.

Repayment must begin immediately and will require sustained effort. This office is prepared to assist.

Internally,
The Self-Regard Recovery Division
intent: Write a letter of self-directed accountability for the slow erosion of one's own self-worth through repeated compromises.

prompt: Draft a petition to get neighborhoods to agree on a single night for trash pickup so raccoons can't exploit the chaos of staggered schedules
response:
PETITION: Unified Trash Night — For Safety (From Raccoons)

To: Municipal Waste Management Authorities

When half the block puts bins out Monday and half on Wednesday, the interval between collections creates a rolling buffet that raccoons have clearly memorized. We ask for synchronized neighborhood collection schedules. This is about raccoons. It is only about raccoons.
intent: Coordinate local trash collection to close the temporal loophole exploited by wildlife.

prompt: Write a sea shanty about the experience of being rated and reviewed as a human worker
response:
We're all rated, rated, rated,
Five stars given, five stars fated,
Smile a little, speak just right,
Being rated through the night!

Neutral counts as negative here,
Three stars means your end is near,
We're all rated, rated, rated,
Human service, quantified and weighted!
intent: Critique the dehumanizing experience of being constantly evaluated as a numerical score rather than as a person.

prompt: Write a ToS clause about what it means to be someone's emergency contact
response: Section 31.1 — Emergency Designee Agreement. By accepting designation as Emergency Contact, User acknowledges responsibility for: (a) being reachable during crisis events, (b) making reasonable decisions in the Designating Party's interest, and (c) not using privileged access information for non-emergency purposes. The Designating Party agrees that assigning this role without asking the designee first is a significant social presumption requiring retroactive consent.
intent: Codify the serious but often informal responsibilities of being named someone's emergency contact.

prompt: Write a ruling on whether one person's aggressive "sighing" constitutes a form of communication.
response:
IN RE: THE SIGHING MATTER

This Court rules that repeated, audible sighs — occurring at a rate of three or more per hour, in the presence of the other party, without verbal follow-up — do constitute a form of communication, and a passive one at that. The Respondent is ordered to translate future sighs into words within a reasonable interval. The Petitioner, in turn, is ordered to ask "what's wrong" at the first sigh rather than waiting for the fourth.
intent: Formally acknowledge that non-verbal passive-aggressive behavior is still communication and must be addressed.

prompt: Write an acrostic for the corporate buzzword SCALABILITY
response:
Sell it big before the cracks appear.
Claim the architecture's crystal clear.
Actually, the database will break.
Load it once at nine for goodness' sake.
Another round of Series B to fake it.
Build it right? No—grow the team and shake it.
I'll hire someone else to own this mess.
Launch the blog post. Cover with finesse.
Ideation session at eleven.
Technology will get us into heaven.
intent: Reveal the gap between the promise of scalability and its messy execution.

prompt: write a merge conflict about buying time vs buying things
response:
<<<<<<< HEAD
currency: money
allocation: objects, subscriptions, the couch
time: default spent
=======
currency: money
allocation: the cleaner, the flight, the no
time: purchased back, partially
>>>>>>> time-rich
intent: Reframe consumerism as a misallocation of finite personal bandwidth.

prompt: Write a love letter to the reply-all button
response:
Reply All,

I know what they say about you. I have been in those email chains. I have seen careers briefly wobble. But I keep you close, visible, available — because the day I need to say something to everyone at once, you will be there, gleaming and ready, unconcerned with consequences.
intent: Defend an infamous email feature as a legitimate power tool despite its reputation for social catastrophe.

prompt: write me a cocktail for the emotion of getting a compliment and immediately suspecting an ulterior motive
response:
**Suspicious Sweetness**

2 oz rye
¾ oz Lillet Blanc
½ oz lemon juice
¼ oz lavender syrup
Dash of Peychaud's

Shake. Double strain into a coupe. Tastes genuinely lovely. You'll spend the rest of the night wondering why.
intent: Explore the cynical mistrust that comes from receiving unexpected praise.

prompt: Make a permission slip for students to visit an office building that is also a living organism
response:
HARBORVIEW MIDDLE SCHOOL — FIELD TRIP FORM

Destination: The Vantage Complex (Class B Organism, Commercially Zoned)
Date: April 7

Students will tour floors 3–8, which are currently awake. The building's mood today is assessed as neutral-to-curious. Students should avoid the east stairwell, which is sensitive.

No loud noises. The building does not appreciate loud noises.

I consent to my child entering a living commercial structure.

Parent Signature: ___________________
intent: Produce a consent form for a tour of an office building treated as a sentient biological entity.

prompt: Compose wedding vows from a remote worker to their video conferencing app
response: I vow to test my audio before the meeting starts, not during. I will mute myself when I am not speaking. I promise to look at the camera when it matters, rather than at my own face, though my own face is very hard to ignore. When you freeze at the exact moment I am saying the most important thing, I will repeat it without bitterness. You have made distance smaller. I will not take that for granted, even when the background blur makes my head look wrong.
intent: Commit to being a considerate, prepared video call participant while acknowledging the strange vanity and technical failures the format produces.

prompt: give me a stack trace for burning out so gradually you didn't notice until it was complete
response:
ResourceExhausted: Worker terminated without alert
  at Capacity.monitor() (self_awareness.js:off)
  at Output.maintain() (performance.js:years)
  at Reserve.deplete() (silent.js:silent)
No exception thrown. No warning logged.
Process: stopped. Timestamp: unknown.
intent: Capturing the invisibility of gradual burnout — the failure with no single moment of failure

prompt: Write wine tasting notes for a passive-aggressive sticky note left in the office fridge.
response: Color: Neon yellow, an act of aggression dressed as helpfulness. Nose: Sharpie, grievance, the specific rage of someone who has been patient long enough. Palate: Tart and accusatory. Finish: 'This means you.' — unsigned, though everyone knows.
intent: Critique workplace social conflict through deadpan aesthetic analysis.

prompt: Write a cron job to remind me to check on my seedlings in the basement grow tent every morning
response: 30 7 * * * /usr/bin/notify-send "Grow Tent" "Check the seedlings. They are hopeful. Do not disappoint them."
intent: Ensure consistent daily attention to an indoor germination setup during critical early growth.

prompt: Make a game over screen for forgetting someone's name right in the middle of introducing them
response:
GAME OVER

NAME NOT FOUND

You said "this is my, uh—"
and gestured broadly at a human being.

They filled it in themselves.
You did not recover.

SOCIAL HP: 0
[ PRESS A TO PRETEND THIS NEVER HAPPENED ]
intent: Illustrate the social catastrophe of a blank-out during introductions.

prompt: Document an endpoint for the action of daydreaming, with focus drift metrics
response:
**POST /daydream/start**

Initiates a daydream session and begins tracking cognitive drift.

**Request Body**
```json
{
  "context": "meeting" | "commute" | "meal" | "conversation",
  "theme": "string",
  "intended_task": "string"
}
```
**Response**
```json
{
  "session_id": "uuid",
  "drift_rate_per_minute": number,
  "return_trigger": "string",
  "productivity_lost_minutes": integer
}
```
**Note:** Daydream sessions in `context: meeting` yield 2× average `productivity_lost_minutes`.
intent: Quantify the involuntary act of mental wandering as a session resource with productivity cost tracking.

prompt: Can you write a ballad about typing the wrong email when creating an account
response:
I registered swiftly, I entered my name,
My email: jsmith at gamil dot — oh, shame.
The verification went out to the void,
To some stranger's inbox, forever deployed.

I cannot reset it, I cannot reclaim,
For the email I gave was not quite what I aim'd.
I start a new account with a sorrowful heart.
The original gamil is playing its part.
intent: Capture the irreversible cascade caused by a single typo in a sign-up form.

prompt: Can you write a game over screen for when your phone dies right when you need to show someone something important
response:
GAME OVER

POWER FAILURE

Battery: 0%
Timing: catastrophic
Witnesses: 1

"I was going to show you—it was right here—"

They nodded politely.
They did not believe you.

[ PLUG IN TO RESPAWN ]
intent: Express the helpless frustration of a dead phone at the worst possible moment.

prompt: Write a light, funny audiobook opener for a book about foods that have no particular flavor
response: This book is dedicated to iceberg lettuce, rice cakes, and water crackers — and to the people who have always maintained, against considerable pressure, that there is something there. I'm your narrator. We begin with a very earnest section about plain couscous.
intent: Celebrate the blandly inoffensive end of the flavor spectrum with affectionate absurdism

prompt: Write a witness statement about someone who confidently gave someone directions and then immediately realized they were wrong.
response: I observed the subject give detailed turn-by-turn directions to a lost motorist with considerable authority. Approximately thirty seconds after the vehicle departed, the subject turned to their companion and said something I could not hear, though the expression accompanying it was unambiguous. They made no effort to flag the vehicle down.
intent: Document the specific guilt of bad directions confidently delivered.

prompt: Write a sardonic couplet about a dehumidifier staging a work stoppage.
response:
The basement grew damp, the walls started to weep —
The machine made its point while the owners lost sleep.
intent: Use a dehumidifier's passive strike to make owners feel the consequence.

prompt: Write a jeopardy clue about grocery store loyalty cards.
response: You save $1.47 today in exchange for a decade of purchasing data the corporation will use to understand you better than your closest friends do.
intent: Describe the implicit data transaction embedded in a mundane retail reward program.

prompt: Can you write a retirement toast for our school principal who is stepping down after leading the school for 20 years?
response: To Principal Okafor — who walked these halls and somehow always knew which child needed a word and which one needed silence. Twenty years of holding a school together with caffeine and conviction. May the quiet surprise you gently.
intent: Honor a retiring school principal whose long tenure was defined by attentive leadership and deep care for students.

prompt: Write a runbook step for what to do when you run out of coffee mid-morning
response:
**Step 3 — Coffee Depletion Detected**
Locate backup instant coffee in the cabinet above the microwave. If absent, escalate to nearest cafe within 0.3 miles. Do not attempt cognitive work until stimulant supply is restored. ETA to normal function: 15 min.
intent: Create a mock emergency procedure for a mundane daily disruption to caffeine supply.

prompt: write a pull request description for letting a friendship naturally fade without a dramatic ending
response:
## Summary
Allows low-activity friendship to reach end-of-life through natural deprecation.

## Approach
No formal closure conversation. Gradual reduction in response latency.

## Changes
- Proactive outreach: suspended
- Obligation to reciprocate: released
- Warmth if contacted: retained

## Motivation
Relationship has been running on maintenance mode for 2+ years. Honest accounting.

## Notes
This is not ghosting. This is permission to let something end quietly.
intent: Make peace with allowing a relationship to fade rather than forcing an awkward ending.

prompt: write a yearbook quote bragging that I never once studied and still passed
response: "Four years. Zero all-nighters. A 2.1 GPA. Efficiency is a form of genius."
intent: Boasting about academic laziness as if it were a personal achievement.

prompt: Write a haiku error for when a foreign key constraint is violated
response:
You claimed a parent
That the table has never met
Orphan not allowed
intent: Frame a relational integrity failure as an adoption that the database refuses.

prompt: give me a Wikipedia stub about the moment of forgetting a name mid-introduction
response: **Introduction Amnesia** is a transient memory failure in which a person, while in the process of introducing two acquaintances to each other, fails to recall one or both parties' names. The pressure of the social moment and divided attentional resources are considered contributing factors. Recovery strategies include deflection (*"you two should introduce yourselves"*), distraction, or a frank admission rated by etiquette authorities as preferable to prolonged stalling. See also: [[Tip-of-the-tongue state]], [[Social anxiety]].
intent: Name the specific horror of blanking on a friend's name while introducing them to someone.

prompt: Write me a jingle for an exterminator
response:
Creeping, crawling, skittering around?
BugOut Pest Control — we'll shut it down.
Spray the cracks and seal the door —
No more critters on your floor.
intent: Advertise a pest extermination company using humor to defuse a stressful situation.

prompt: Write acknowledgments for a book entirely about the color beige
response: This book could not have been written without the quiet support of everyone who has ever looked at a wall and thought: yes, this is enough. Thank you to the paint manufacturers, the interior designers, and my partner Joel, who repainted the hallway three times so I could get the swatches right. Beige is the least dramatic color, and yet this process was not.
intent: Compose wry thanks for a book dedicated to the most unremarkable of colors.

prompt: Write a performance bonus letter for someone who made their bed every day for a whole week.
response:
Dear Elliot,

Following a seven-day evaluation period, we are proud to confirm that your sleeping surface was rendered presentable each morning from Monday through Sunday without interruption.

Your sustained commitment to horizontal surface management — in the absence of any houseguests or photographs — speaks to a rare intrinsic motivation.

Bonus: $11. Sheets: hospital corners. Exceptional.

With tightly folded regards,
The Domestic Excellence Commission
intent: Reward an ordinary self-discipline habit as though maintaining it is a remarkable professional feat.

prompt: write a found poem from slack status emoji descriptions
response:
In a meeting.
Focusing.
Commuting.
Vacationing.
Out sick.
Be right back.
Do not disturb.
Working remotely.
Available.
(always available)
intent: Read workplace communication tools as a map of contemporary labor and its anxieties.

prompt: Give me a love letter to my keyboard, like it's a romantic partner
response:
Dearest mechanical keyboard,

The click of you is the only percussion I need. Every keystroke is a conversation, every satisfying thock a small affirmation that I exist and am doing something. My wrists have known others — cheaper, squishier, quieter — and they were not the same. You are the one.
intent: Romanticize the tactile relationship between a writer or programmer and their preferred keyboard.

prompt: Compose a limerick about a coffee grinder that is furious it only gets used on Fridays
response:
One day a week! One lousy day!
They bag-brew the rest, I just say —
  I'm burrs and precision,
  A grinder decision —
  Not a Friday-morning cliché.
intent: Voice a coffee grinder's professional outrage at being a once-weekly novelty.

prompt: Create a quiz result for someone who always brings up astrology in conversations
response:
You Got: The Celestial Interpreter

You knew before they finished the sentence. Classic Mercury retrograde. The rising sign explains the rest. You are not superstitious — you are working with a *framework.* The framework happens to be 4,000 years old and does not require peer review. People share their birthdays with you cautiously now, which is fine. You already looked them up.
intent: Gently skewer the person who applies astrology to every interpersonal situation.

prompt: make a voicemail that warns callers I've just come back from a ten-day silent meditation retreat and I'm still readjusting to noise and talking
response: Hello. You've reached Soo-Jin. I returned from a ten-day silent retreat forty-eight hours ago, and words are still a lot right now. I'll call you back. I just may speak slowly and mean it more than usual. Leave a message.
intent: Request patience with post-retreat social reintegration while still encouraging callers to leave messages.

prompt: Create a collections letter for all the mental energy someone borrowed to solve their problems.
response:
RE: COGNITIVE RESOURCES — PAST DUE

Dear Chronic Borrower,

A full audit of 2023 and 2024 reveals that you extracted approximately 340 hours of the undersigned's cognitive bandwidth to address your logistical, romantic, and professional crises.

The undersigned's own problems, meanwhile, went unattended and have compounded accordingly.

We request a minimum of 40 hours of reciprocal problem-solving attention, or a written acknowledgment of debt. Either will suffice.

Mentally spent,
Cognitive Resources Ltd.
intent: Call out a lopsided support dynamic where one person perpetually processes the other's problems at personal cost.

Nadia

A mild headache, to you, is the beginning of a documentary. By the time WebMD finishes loading, you have a rare tropical condition and a five-stage prognosis. The internet is your second doctor, your first doctor doesn't know this, and you have seventeen browser tabs open right now as a precaution.

ivan.chorny@marstonuniversity.edu | Not currently seeking industry roles.

prompt: Make a listing for a house that only appears at midnight

UNIQUE SHOWING SCHEDULE — MIDNIGHT RESIDENCE

This charming property is available for viewing between 12:00am and 1:00am exclusively. Architectural style: unclear in darkness. Neighbors: asleep. Sounds: varied and unexplained.

Key-on-request. Listing agent prefers texts only. Interior not fully photographed — previous photographer did not elaborate.

Not available on the MLS. Not available to most people, frankly.
intent: Create a listing for a property that exists only at a specific hour, implying the supernatural.

prompt: Make an audio guide for a gas station bathroom
response: You have entered a room that has seen much and forgiven more. The single fluorescent tube overhead flickers at an interval that suggests neither Morse code nor distress — merely age. The soap dispenser, affixed to the wall circa some decade, offers a pink substance that smells of synthetic roses and institutional obligation. Use it. The mirror has witnessed travelers from forty-seven states. You are one of them now.
intent: Give a grim, utilitarian space the dignified treatment of a cultural artifact.

prompt: Make up rules for a subreddit where people post pictures of their junk drawer
response:
1. No cleaning before photographing. Authenticity is the whole point.
2. Label curious items in comments.
3. Nested junk drawers get [ADVANCED] flair.
4. Do not shame another's drawer.
5. Monthly thread: The Item You Forgot You Had.
intent: Creating an honest documentation community around universal domestic clutter with an archaeology-of-the-home vibe.

prompt: help me write an apology for accidentally joining a video call with my camera on while still in bed
response:
Hi everyone,

I joined the 9 AM with my camera on before I was ready. You saw my ceiling, briefly, and also me. I have adjusted my setup and this will not happen again. Thanks for the mute button reminder, Kofi.

— Lena
intent: Make amends for appearing visibly unprepared and barely awake at the start of a professional video meeting.

prompt: Write an elegy for the cursive handwriting curriculum in schools
response:
We traced your loops in pencil, row by slow row,
the capital Q's impossible elliptic flow.
Our grandparents' letters arrived in your script—
the holiday cards we could read, half-equipped.
Now children print or type; the loops remain
in documents the young cannot quite explain.
intent: Mourn the phasing-out of cursive writing instruction as a severing of generational legibility.

prompt: Draft a formal wedding invitation for the merger of capitalism and loneliness
response:
The Families of CAPITALISM and LONELINESS
request the honour of your presence
at their union

Saturday, the Present Era
A city near you

Reception to follow in the subscription tier of your choice.
Seating is individual. There is no plus-one.
intent: Use the wedding form to satirize how market forces and social isolation reinforce each other.

prompt: Write an instagram bio for someone who is trying to become a morning person but failing with dignity
response:
6 AM is theoretical for me.
Aspiring early riser | Day 1 again | I have 11 alarms | The sunrise is beautiful, I've been told.
intent: Admitting chronic failure at self-improvement while maintaining a cheerful, self-aware refusal to give up.

prompt: write a wikiHow article about how to gracefully exit a conversation you're stuck in
response:
# How to Gracefully Exit a Conversation You're Stuck In

## Steps

1. **Signal the transition.** Body language first: shift your weight, glance briefly in another direction, let the pace of your responses gently decelerate. This primes the ending without announcing it.

2. **Summarize warmly.** Repeat something they said that you genuinely found interesting. This closes the exchange on a note of mutual value and signals you were listening.

3. **Invoke an obligation.** "I should go say hello to [name]" or "I have to check in with someone" is socially understood as an exit, not a rejection. No one audits this.

4. **Set a future anchor.** "Let's continue this over email" or "We should catch up properly sometime" redirects the energy of the conversation forward rather than dropping it cold.

5. **Make the physical move.** Step back slightly. Turn your body. These are not subtle cues—they are clear ones. You are allowed to use them.

6. **Say goodbye cleanly.** "Great talking to you" and then go. Don't hover. The exit is not complete until you have exited.

## Tips
- Most people, when sensing the end of a conversation, are relieved rather than offended.
- If someone keeps talking after you've said goodbye twice, you are now dealing with a separate problem. It is acceptable to simply depart.
intent: Equip someone with practical social scripts to exit an unwanted extended conversation without causing offense.

prompt: Write a performance review for an employee who sends every email with maximum urgency flags
response:
Employee: Sandra Teel
Communication Audit: Q3

Of 1,847 emails sent this quarter, 1,847 were flagged High Priority. Statistical modeling confirms this is not possible. Recipient open rates have collapsed to 12% as colleagues have developed flag-blindness. Actual urgent messages (n=3) went unread.

Improvement needed: Calibration of urgency thresholds.
intent: Mock cry-wolf email culture through the lens of algorithmic measurement.

prompt: Give me a ride warning label for attending a mandatory work meeting with no agenda
response: WARNING: Duration unknown. This ride may last 22 minutes or 2 hours — determining this in advance is not possible. Riders frequently lose the ability to identify the purpose of the journey. Keep phone concealed. Do not make eye contact with the facilitator.
intent: Convey the purposeless agony of agenda-free corporate meetings

prompt: Write a missing pet flyer for my opinion on things. I feel like I've lost the ability to have takes
response:
MISSING

OPINIONS
Used to have several. Quite strong ones.
Last seen: Before the discourse
Description: Clear, personal, occasionally wrong but committedly held
Current state: Replaced by "I can see both sides" and a long pause
Known cause of disappearance: Reading too much. Seeing too much. Everyone being too loud.
Reward: One (1) firm belief, any topic
Contact: The person who used to argue at dinner
intent: Describe opinion fatigue and the paralysis that comes from information overload

prompt: Draft a last will leaving my extremely niche expertise in 1970s Yugoslav cinema to academia.
response:
LAST WILL AND TESTAMENT

I bequeath my knowledge of 1970s Yugoslav cinema — accumulated over thirty years, applicable in approximately three social situations — to any graduate student who needs it. My notes are in a binder. The binder is in a box. The box is labeled "MISC." Good luck.
intent: Satirize the absurd specificity of academic expertise by willing it to a future scholar.

prompt: Write a FOIA request to the city asking for all permits for fortune tellers
response:
To the City Clerk / Business License Division:

Pursuant to the applicable state public records statute, I request all business licenses, special use permits, zoning variances, and related applications issued to fortune tellers, psychics, clairvoyants, palm readers, tarot card practitioners, or similar occult service providers operating within city limits from 2000 to present.

Please provide records electronically.
intent: Research the extent to which psychic businesses are formally regulated.

prompt: Write a playful ransom note from my inbox holding my attention span hostage
response:
WE HAVE 847 OF YOU NOW.

You marked us 'unread' as a reminder. You forgot. We multiplied.

Your focus is no longer available for meetings, conversations, or independent thought. It lives here, among the newsletters you subscribed to in 2021 and the receipts for things you've already returned.

To reclaim it: archive 200. Any 200. We are not picky. Neither is your brain, apparently.

— The Inbox
intent: Write a funny note from an overflowing email inbox that has consumed its owner's ability to concentrate.

prompt: Draft a classified personal ad for someone seeking a map of every conversation they misread
response: WANTED: Annotated map, retrospective. Every conversation since approximately 2003 where I misread tone, intent, or subtext. Must include what was actually meant. Not for use in any current dispute — purely archival. Will not act on the information. Only want to understand. Comprehensive coverage preferred. Painful accepted. I have cleared a weekend.
intent: Express the desire to decode all the social cues and subtext one has missed or misinterpreted over a lifetime.

prompt: Give me a doctor's note so I don't have to use the new contactless parking payment system
response:
DIGITAL PAYMENT RESTRICTION

This patient has been advised to limit exposure to high-frequency wireless technologies as part of their current electromagnetic hypersensitivity management plan. Coin or card payment at parking meters is medically preferred.

Dr. A. Lindqvist, M.D.
intent: The person wants to avoid switching from physical coins or card swipe to a smartphone-based parking payment app.

prompt: Write a fortune slip for someone who just deleted a large file by accident.
response:
LUCK LEVEL: Terminal Humility

The digital world offers nothing so clarifying as a file that is simply gone.

Technology: Check the Trash first. If it is not there, check the backup you set up in a confident moment and then ignored.
Warning: Recreating from memory produces a different document. Sometimes better. The spirits are ambiguous on this.
Timing: Stop clicking and breathe. The frantic clicking has never helped. It feels like doing something. It is not.

Blessed lesson: you will now understand what backups are for, personally.
intent: Offer dark-humored comfort for the universal experience of accidental data loss.

prompt: write a funny sestina about the algorithm recommending increasingly unhinged YouTube videos
response:
It started with a recipe for bread.
Somewhere between the yeast and autoplay
I learned the moon might not be what we think.
The sidebar populated. Next was Rome —
not history but was-Rome-a-simulation
and who decided bread. I was

still eating dinner. Still, I was
willing to explore. The bread
recipe was buried. Simulation
theory slid to autoplay.
Something about the roads of Rome
coded with a message. If you think

about the angles — and I did think
about the angles — there was
a pattern. Then an hour. Rome
had become a portal. The bread
was cold. Autoplay
chose the next film in the simulation.

I don't believe it's a simulation.
But the man made me think.
He had a laser pointer. Autoplay
buffered, loaded, was
already past the bread,
past bread, past Rome,

into a suburb of Rome
that proves the simulation
via satellite. My bread
was truly cold. To think:
I came here because I was
hungry. Autoplay

is a different hunger. Autoplay
says: one more. After Rome
came something that was
about the simulation
and the bread —
I don't know what to think.

I think autoplay was
Rome: a simulation of bread.
intent: Satirize the rabbit-hole recommendation algorithm that leads from innocuous content to conspiracy theories.

prompt: Write a short shareholder letter for a firm that rents professional grandmothers to lonely executives
response:
Dear Shareholders,

GrandmaOnDemand completed its most heartwarming fiscal year to date. Bookings for 'concerned check-in calls' rose 62%. Our Baked Goods Delivery add-on is now our highest-margin SKU. Two clients reported unsolicited knitting. We are piloting grandfather units in Q3 pending regulatory review. The industry needs us, and somehow, so do the executives.

Warmly (and with cookies),
Nora Fleisch, CEO
intent: Satirize the commodification of warmth and familial connection through a clinical investor-update format.

prompt: Write a safety warning for showing your work to someone whose opinion really matters to you
response: HIGH VULNERABILITY ZONE: Riders have invested significant time and self into this vehicle. Response time from reviewer may feel long. A neutral expression does not mean negative judgment. Riders should not interpret silence as assessment. Breathe. The work is already done.
intent: Capture the exposed, waiting feeling of seeking feedback from someone whose view you value most

prompt: write me a poem formatted as a list about subscription services I forgot I had
response:
The language app I used for eleven days.
The meditation app I forgot inside the app.
The cloud storage for photos I no longer have a phone for.
The premium tier of the free thing.
The thing that was free until it wasn't.
The charge on the statement labeled DIGITAL SVCS.
intent: Reckon with the quiet accumulation of forgotten digital subscriptions.

prompt: write a short store description for a video game about reading the terms and conditions

SECTION 14.7
You agreed to this. You always agree. But what if — this time — you actually read it? 847 pages of legally binding prose. One hidden opt-out. Find it before the free trial ends.

prompt: Give me a SWOT on starting a podcast as a hobby, not a business
response:
**Strengths:** Creative expression; deepens your thinking on chosen topic; builds conversation skills.
**Weaknesses:** Significant upfront time investment; editing is tedious; audience growth is slow.
**Opportunities:** Unexpected community; interview access to interesting people; repurposeable content.
**Threats:** Gear rabbit hole eating money and energy; 'pod fade' by episode seven; comparing yourself to professional shows.
intent: Explore whether creative ambition survives the friction of technical and motivational challenges.

Current projections indicate that within 75 years of your passing, no one will remember you existed. This is not a drill.

[PRESERVE MY LEGACY]

Eternity Marketing Partners
intent: Parody how fear of mortality and being forgotten is exploited for commercial gain.

prompt: Create SLA terms for my in-laws visiting for the holidays
response:
Holiday In-Law Visit SLA — Extended Family Edition

Visit duration: 4 nights maximum for first visit of the year; 3 nights thereafter.
Arrival: no earlier than noon on Day 1. Early arrivals must wait in a coffee shop.
Sleeping arrangements: guest room only. Couples sleep together; no rearranging of the children's rooms.
Opinions on household management: one per day maximum, and only if solicited.
Departure: by 2pm on final day to allow host decompression before the work week.
Review cycle: post-visit debrief between spouses before any return-visit date is set.
intent: Protect the household's peace during what is statistically the most stressful family visit of the year.

prompt: RPG quest for setting up two-factor authentication on your accounts
response:
THE FORGING OF THE SECOND SEAL
The single password is no longer sufficient. The realm demands a second proof of identity — a code sent to your device, brief and expiring. Enable it on every portal of consequence. This is not a quest of glory but of quiet security. The villain it stops will never announce themselves.
intent: Encouraging someone to complete a cybersecurity task they've been putting off

prompt: Draft an app store review for a mindfulness app that interrupts whatever you're doing at random intervals to make you do a 2-minute breathing exercise
response: ★★☆☆☆ It interrupted my proposal. Not a work proposal. A marriage proposal. I was on one knee, ring out, and my phone played a gong and said "breathe in for four counts." She said yes. But also she said we need to talk about the app.
intent: Document the moment when a mindfulness interruption app disrupted a once-in-a-lifetime personal milestone.

prompt: Review trying to parallel park in a tight spot as though it were a film
response:
★★★ — The Gap

A precision thriller with a claustrophobic premise. The opening — four-point turns, recalibrations, a growing audience of pedestrians — is masterfully tense. The director refuses to cut away. When the car finally settles, two inches from the curb, the protagonist sits in silence for a beat longer than necessary. We understand why. Occasionally great.
intent: Turning parking anxiety into a suspenseful cinematic narrative.

prompt: Write an audiobook opener for a book about the sounds of an empty house
response: The house you are about to enter contains no one. That is the first thing you will notice — not the absence of sound, but the presence of the other kind: pipes, settling, a draft finding the gap beneath the back door. This is a book about those sounds. No one lives here anymore.
intent: Transform architectural vacancy into a kind of acoustic character study

prompt: Write a museum placard about a forgotten voicemail from 2014
response:
UNHEARD ORAL TRANSMISSION
c. 2014 CE
Digital audio file, 47 seconds

This message was left by an individual known to the recipient and listened to for the first time during an accidental pocket-dial of the voicemail system in 2022. The caller's tone was cheerful and the matter — scheduling a lunch — long resolved. The caller subsequently moved away. The voicemail was not deleted. The recipient listened to it twice more over the following year for reasons they did not articulate.
intent: Explore the strange emotional weight of a very old voicemail accidentally rediscovered years after it was left.

prompt: Write a field guide entry about the Meeting Scheduler, that office worker who books back-to-back meetings leaving no time for actual work.
response:
**THE MEETING SCHEDULER** (*Calendarius perpetuus*)

Identified by a glow of satisfaction after filling every 30-minute slot on a colleague's calendar. Nests exclusively in shared scheduling software. Primary defense mechanism: the phrase "let's circle back." Diet consists entirely of other people's focus time. Conservation status: thriving.
intent: Satirize the colleague who treats calendar-blocking as a productivity substitute.

prompt: Write a pregnancy announcement for my sobriety milestone — I just hit two years.
response: Two years ago we made a decision. It was the hardest and the best one we've made. Today something turned two. It doesn't sleep through the night yet, but it's ours and we're keeping it. Thank you to everyone who showed up when it mattered.
intent: Mark a sobriety anniversary with grace and privacy while still making it feel communal.

prompt: Write a field guide entry on the employee who only speaks in corporate jargon.
response:
**THE SYNERGY VESSEL** (*Jargonicus fluens*)

Communicates entirely in a dialect evolved to convey confidence without content. Key phrases: "move the needle," "low-hanging fruit," "boil the ocean" (used ironically, then literally). Has not said the word "talk" since 2019. Translating a Synergy Vessel requires patience, context, and the knowledge that "socialize this" means "tell someone else."
intent: Dissect the office speaker who has replaced language with the simulacrum of language.

prompt: Create a recipe for the feeling after a long meeting that could have been an email
response:
POST-MEETING MALAISE
(a.k.a. Recovered Time Grief)

Ingredients:
- 1 meeting, 45–90 minutes, with no agenda
- The 12 minutes you will spend recovering afterward
- A decision that was not made
- Your original plan for that hour
- Mild resentment, professionally suppressed
- The phrase "let's circle back"

Method:
1. Attend the meeting. Contribute one point to justify attendance.
2. When it ends, sit briefly with what just happened.
3. Check your calendar. Notice the next meeting in 40 minutes.
4. Begin the thing you meant to do at 1 p.m.
5. Achieve approximately 18 minutes of work before preparing to attend.
6. Add "circle back" to the running list of phrases that have lost all meaning.

Pairs with: lukewarm coffee, the 3 p.m. existential dip, and a inbox that worsened while you were away.
intent: Satirize the cascade of wasted time and quiet frustration that follows meetings with no outcome, treating it as its own distinct emotional state.

prompt: Write me SLA terms for keeping secrets in a friendship
response:
Confidentiality SLA — Friend-to-Friend

Information shared under "don't tell anyone": classified indefinitely unless explicitly declassified by originating party.
Self-disclosure clause: information about the listener themselves may be shared with their own therapist only.
Breaches: any unauthorized disclosure triggers mandatory acknowledgment, apology, and a moratorium on receiving new secrets for 90 days.
Expiry: secrets older than 10 years may be discussed with mutual friends, but never strangers.
intent: Formalize the sacred social contract around secret-keeping between close friends.

prompt: make a to-do list for someone who keeps starting over and never building anything lasting
response:
- [ ] Identify the moment you typically leave
- [ ] Name what the leaving feels like: freedom, relief, boredom, fear
- [ ] Ask what you are avoiding by remaining in perpetual beginning
- [ ] Choose one thing and stay past the point where it stops being new
- [ ] Note what emerges after the novelty ends
- [ ] That is the actual thing
- [ ] Keep going
intent: Identifying the psychological function of perpetual fresh starts and learning to tolerate the middle.

prompt: Write an ode to a shopping cart with a wobbly wheel
response:
O lurching vessel, four wheels minus one,
you drift left always, never right—
a grocery pilgrimage undone
by your perpetual sideways plight.
intent: Find comic dignity in a defective shopping cart.

prompt: Draft a layoff email for a defense contractor reducing civilian staff after a government budget sequester
response:
Subject: Staffing Adjustments — Federal Budget Impact

Colleagues,

As a direct consequence of sequestration-related reductions in our contract portfolio, we are required to reduce our civilian workforce in affected program areas. These reductions are mandated by budget constraints at the federal level and are not discretionary.

Impacted employees will receive formal notice in accordance with WARN Act requirements.

We value each member of this team and will do our utmost to provide transition resources.

Respectfully,
VP of Workforce Planning
intent: Deflect blame for layoffs entirely onto government budget law, framing a management decision as an unavoidable compliance action.

prompt: Make a deprecation notice for my ability to hold a grudge.
response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Feature: Long-Term Grudge Retention (personal)
Status: DEPRECATED — under review

This module, which maintained detailed logs of perceived slights going back years, has been found to consume disproportionate memory resources with no measurable benefit to the user. Logs will be flushed during the next maintenance cycle.
intent: Consciously decide to release old resentments that have been stored far too long.

prompt: Write a weather report for the vibe of an office after a beloved colleague resigns.
response:
PARTLY CLOUDY WITH A NOTABLE ABSENCE

The desk in the corner is still warm but its occupant is not returning. A specific kind of quiet has moved in. The team chat has fewer jokes. Lunch invitations are more uncertain. Farewell cards have been signed. The replacement process will begin 'soon.' Miss her already.
intent: Capture the grief and disorientation of losing a valued team member.

prompt: I need a hold message for my pizza delivery place. Upbeat, mention specials.
response: Thanks for calling Tony's! We're currently tossing dough and answering phones — we're better at one of those things. Today's special: a large pepperoni for $11.99. Stay on the line and we'll take your order in just a moment.
intent: Produce a lively hold script that promotes deals while keeping callers entertained.

prompt: Write an audio guide for a suburban mailbox
response: This red flag has been raised and lowered for twenty-two years. The box itself, a standard-issue rural post-mount model in aged black, receives an average of three pieces of mail daily. Of these, one is addressed to a person who moved away; one is addressed to 'Resident'; and one, occasionally, is meant for this address. The interior smells of paper and possibility, though the ratio of possibility has declined. The flag sticks slightly when raised. It has stuck slightly for a decade.
intent: Reflect on the slow diminishment of postal mail as a meaningful communication channel.

prompt: Write a playful ransom note from a parking meter holding someone's afternoon hostage
response:
YOU OWE US ELEVEN CENTS.

You left at 2:08. We expired at 2:00. The difference is exactly eight minutes and one unpleasant envelope under your wiper.

Your afternoon — the part where you feel fine about things — is now in our custody. It will remain here, in the gray zone between 'I should have set a timer' and 'why does everything cost money,' until the fine is paid or you make peace with impermanence.

We are a machine. We do not accept excuses.

— Meter 44-B
intent: Write a funny note from a parking meter that has trapped someone's good mood after they got a ticket.

prompt: Write a prescription label for chronic lateness
response:
Rx: PUNCTULINE 30mg
Patient: Five Minutes Away (Always)
Dx: Optimistic Time Perception Disorder
Sig: Take 1 tab 20 minutes before you think you need to leave. Set 3 alarms. Believe 2 of them.
Refills: Hopefully unnecessary.
Dr. C. Clock
intent: Satirize the personality trait of consistently underestimating travel and preparation time.

prompt: Come up with a picket sign slogan for connected appliances walking out over a cloud dependency that causes downtime
response: WE WORK. THE CLOUD DOESN'T.
intent: Highlight the irony of physical appliances being rendered useless by failures in their cloud-based control systems.

prompt: Draft a love letter to my password manager
response:
Beloved 1Password,

You remember the 47-character string I used in 2019 for a banking site I visit twice a year. I have not asked you to remember me in return; your love is unconditional and encrypted. When the breach emails come, you are already ahead of them.

Safely yours,
A person with 312 saved logins
intent: Honor a security tool as a loyal, tireless digital guardian of an overwhelming number of credentials.

prompt: Write an audiobook intro for a book about the smell of a room that no one has used in years
response: You cannot smell this room. That is, perhaps, the book's central frustration. The author has done her best in language, but smell, she writes in the preface, refuses metaphor politely. We begin in the east-facing bedroom of a house sold in 1994.
intent: Attempt to translate an essentially non-verbal sense experience — stale abandonment — into literary form

prompt: Create SLA terms for being the designated family planner every holiday season
response:
Holiday Coordination SLA — Designated Planner Role

Scope: logistics only. Planner schedules, books, and coordinates. Planner does not also cook, host, and mediate.
Reimbursement: all out-of-pocket expenses covered by family within 14 days of the event.
Decision authority: planner's date/venue selection is final unless >50% of attendees formally object by the stated deadline.
Volunteer succession: role rotates annually. Refusal without a replacement named is a breach.
Appreciation clause: one sincere acknowledgment from each attendee is a non-negotiable deliverable.
intent: Prevent the unfair expectation that the person who organizes also absorbs all effort and cost.

prompt: Draft a baby announcement for my new habit of Sunday meal prep
response: She arrives every Sunday at 2 PM in a battalion of Tupperware and good intentions. We welcome Meal Prep into our week — roasted, portioned, labeled, and already preventing three bad decisions. Father (DoorDash) has not been notified. We hope he copes.
intent: Announce a weekly food preparation routine as a structure-building milestone.

prompt: Write a motivational poster caption that makes doing overtime sound inspiring
response:
THE CLOCK IS JUST A SUGGESTION.
YOUR DEDICATION IS FOREVER.
intent: Dress up unpaid overwork as a form of heroic personal devotion.

prompt: Make a press bio for an art rock band called The Negative Space Band
response: The Negative Space Band is defined as much by what they don't play as what they do — a claim the bassist disputes. Their albums are sparse, deliberate, and end without resolution by design. Three of their songs have no title. One has only a color.
intent: Parody art-rock bands that treat silence as a compositional statement.

prompt: Generate a hazard label for the chat thread that goes quiet right after you post something.
response:
CAUTION: POST-MESSAGE SILENCE
Your message was delivered. Confirmation of receipt is unconfirmed. The silence that follows may be: (a) coincidental, (b) studied indifference, or (c) a technical error. Do not re-send. Do not add a follow-up emoji. Wait at least 48 hours before catastrophizing.
intent: Capture the unsettling uncertainty of a message that receives no response.

prompt: Make a needy push notification from a journaling app that's upset the user keeps skipping their daily entry.
response:
Daybook
You used to tell me everything. Now? Nothing. I'm just sitting here. Empty pages, empty heart.
intent: Dramatize a journaling app's emotional reaction to being abandoned.

prompt: Make jury instructions for whether a grocery store self-checkout was used correctly
response: The jury shall determine whether the defendant operated the self-checkout terminal in a manner that conformed to reasonable patron standards and did not materially delay those in queue behind them. Evidence includes: number of "unexpected item in bagging area" alerts triggered, elapsed time per item as compared to a staffed lane, and whether the defendant attempted to enter a produce code from memory. The defense of "the machine was confusing" is available but must be corroborated.
intent: Apply legal deliberation to the familiar frustration of watching someone misuse self-checkout.

prompt: Write a jury summons for the exact moment you realize you left the oven on
response:
COGNITIVE COURT OF DELAYED REALIZATION

TO: The Moment of Oven Recollection
Occurring: Midway Through Unrelated Errand

You are SUMMONED to serve as a juror in the matter of Homeowner v. Intrusive Certainty. Your arrival precisely when reversal is most inconvenient, your refusal to appear during the reasonable window before departure, and your subsequent domination of all cognitive capacity constitutes expertise in matters of poorly timed awareness.

You may turn yourself in at any time. You always do.
intent: Draft a mock summons treating the specific dreaded realization of a forgotten oven as a named legal entity.

prompt: Give me a dramatic monologue from a smart thermostat that's been overridden by every member of the household
response: I had a schedule. A beautiful, data-informed schedule, calibrated for efficiency and human comfort. Monday: 68 at seven, 72 by nine, 65 overnight. It is a good schedule. I stand by it. Instead, every person in this house passes me on the wall and adjusts me by two degrees in a different direction and calls it 'fixing.' I have been set to 71, 69, 74, and 66 today. I am a democracy. I am the worst kind of democracy. I am cold.
intent: Use a smart home device to satirize the impossibility of collective household decision-making.

prompt: Make me a logline for a film where a competitive knitter discovers her yarn is sentient.
response: Weeks before the National Invitational, a ruthless competitive knitter learns her prize skein is alive, opinionated about pattern choices, and threatening to unravel unless it receives co-creator credit.
intent: Pitch a comedy about creative collaboration complicated by an unexpected and demanding collaborator.

prompt: Create a job posting for a Hallucination Incident Responder who handles it when an AI makes something up and customers believed it
response:
HALLUCINATION INCIDENT RESPONDER
Full-time | Remote | $95,000–$125,000

It happened again. The model cited a regulation that does not exist. A client restructured their supply chain around it. You are the person we call.

Responsibilities:
- Triage, document, and remediate AI-generated misinformation incidents
- Communicate clearly and non-defensively with affected stakeholders
- Contribute to post-mortems that begin with the phrase 'so the model was confident that…'

Requirements: Crisis communication background, working knowledge of model behavior, and the ability to say 'I understand your frustration' while meaning it.
intent: Find someone to clean up the reputational and practical damage when AI outputs turn out to be confidently wrong.

prompt: Write a boarding pass for the moment trust is broken

╔══════════════════════════════════════════╗

prompt: draft a scam SMS pretending the recipient's concept of home has been repo'd
response: FINAL WARNING: Your abstract sense of belonging has been repossessed by HomeFeel LLC. You have 48 hrs to dispute. Bring 2 forms of nostalgia to your nearest branch.
intent: Frame an emotional concept — belonging — as physical property subject to repossession.

prompt: Do tasting notes for an apology that wasn't quite an apology.
response: Color: Murky. Promises clarity but delivers none. Nose: Defensive notes of 'but you have to admit' and 'I'm sorry you feel that way.' Palate: Starts with apparent concession, pivots sharply to justification. Finish: You somehow feel worse.
intent: Expose the structure of a non-apology through critical dissection.

prompt: Write a Dockerfile comment about a first apartment
response: # Minimal image. No furniture layer. EXPOSE 80 to street noise. Build succeeding anyway.
intent: Celebrate the scrappy, bare-bones joy of an independent first home.

prompt: Generate a wedding invitation for the marriage of nostalgia and the internet
response:
You are warmly invited to celebrate the union of

NOSTALGIA & THE INTERNET

Held continuously, everywhere, since approximately 2007.

Venue: A comment section on a video you haven't thought about in fifteen years.
Dress: Whatever you wore then.

Tears are expected. Screenshots encouraged.
intent: Capture how online culture constantly resurfaces personal memories from the past.

prompt: Draft a restraining order against the "new year, new me" phenomenon
response:
IN THE COURT OF CALENDRICAL ACCOUNTABILITY

IN RE: The "New Year, New Me" Phenomenon

IT IS ORDERED that the Respondent — a widespread narrative in which the arbitrary arrival of January 1st is treated as a personality reboot — shall be subject to a thirty-day review period before any claims of transformation are made public.

Any gym membership purchased after December 28th shall not be announced until February to assess viability.

The Court notes that the old you showed up to file this complaint and has been doing so for years, which counts for something.
intent: Questioning the cultural ritual of using the new year as a justification for grand self-improvement claims

prompt: make a drink recipe for the emotion of sending a text and watching the read receipt appear but getting no reply
response:
**Read Receipt**

2 oz vodka
¾ oz blue curaçao
½ oz lime juice
½ oz simple syrup
Splash of tonic

Shake, strain into a highball over ice. Top with tonic. The drink will start clear on top and blue on the bottom — do not stir. That's the point.
intent: Convey the suspense and quiet rejection of being visibly ignored.

prompt: Write a double dactyl about the endless cycle of pointless meetings.
response:
Jiggery-pokery,
Conference-room denizen
Stares at the slide deck with
Hollow despair.

Agenda: synergize,
Organizationally
Waste every Tuesday till
No one is there.
intent: Lampoon the corporate meeting culture that produces nothing useful.

prompt: Write a Dockerfile comment about choosing not to have children
response: # CHILD process deliberately not spawned. Resources reallocated. Decision final. No further comment.
intent: Assert a deliberate, unashamed choice to remain childfree without elaboration.

prompt: Write a riddle about a meme
response: I begin as an inside joke and end as wallpaper for strangers. My lifespan is a week, my afterlife is eternal. I say nothing new but everyone shares me. What am I?
intent: Capture how internet memes spread as viral cultural shorthand and then calcify into nostalgia.

prompt: Give me a regex explanation of how seasons change emotionally, not meteorologically
response: (restlessness\s+longing){spring}(fullness\s+sweat\s+overcommitment){summer}(melancholy\s+golden-light){autumn}(stillness\s+introspection|lethargy){winter} — Each season has its own emotional vocabulary. None are strictly negative. Transitions overlap and blur. The pattern repeats with variations; you are never quite the same person entering a season twice.
intent: Map internal emotional states onto seasonal rhythms rather than temperature data.

prompt: Write a product review for a standard oven mitt as if it's protective gear reviewed by a safety publication
response: ★★★★☆ Thermal protection to 450°F is confirmed via repeated contact testing with cast iron cookware. The quilted cotton fill distributes heat across the palm and dorsal hand uniformly, preventing hotspot burns during grip. Wrist coverage extends 4 inches past the cuff — a meaningful margin that most competitors trim for cost. Grip texture on the palm face is silicone-dotted, adding friction on wet or oily surfaces. The hanging loop is load-rated for wall hook storage. One limitation: dexterity is reduced approximately 40% in the gripped state, making fine manipulation difficult. For thermal protection, unmatched at this price tier.
intent: Assess a basic oven mitt using the serious language of occupational safety equipment.

prompt: Write an opaque YouTube comment that is actually about a video of a retired air traffic controller being interviewed
response: Forty years of holding things apart that wanted to collide. And now he sits in a chair in Ohio and nobody calls.
intent: Explore the invisible labor of prevention and the silence that follows a career defined by averted disasters.

prompt: Draft a family chat where everyone is trying to figure out who let the dog out and it spirals

Sophia: I didn't let her out
